behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert specific patterns like 'TaskCacheStats::task_name(inner)' (test_task_name_from_storage checks for TaskCacheStats::task_name, task_name(inner), etc.), 'FormatSizes' struct with Display impl (test_format_sizes_struct), and 'print_cache_item_size_with_compressed' feature name (test_cargo_feature_split). While instruction.md hints at some of these (e.g., 'read from this reference directly'), it never names 'TaskCacheStats::task_name' as the required method name. The test also checks for

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md essentially prescribes the fix: 'Replace all .entry(self.get_task_name(task_id, turbo_tasks)) calls... with entries derived from the storage reference directly', 'Not use self. in any .entry() argument', 'Make print_cache_item_size a standalone feature with no lzzzz dependency', 'Add a separate feature (e.g., print_cache_item_size_with_compressed)', 'Change the guard to encode_data || encode_meta'. These are specific implementation directives, not just descriptions of what's broke

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: test_task_name_from_storage checks for patterns like 'TaskCacheStats::task_name' and 'task_name(inner)' or 'task_name(storage)' — an alternative fix using a different method name (e.g., 'get_name_from_storage') would fail. test_format_sizes_struct checks for 'FormatSizes' by exact name and 'impl' + 'Display' + 'FormatSizes'. An alternative using a different struct name would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Every test uses regex/string matching on source files (e.g., re.findall on .entry() calls, checking for 'FormatSizes' in source, parsing Cargo.toml as text). No test compiles, runs, or executes the Rust code. All tests are pattern-matching on file contents with no behavioral execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: The tests check for non-trivial conditions: .entry() calls must exist but not reference self.get_task_name, task_name must reference storage/inner patterns, Cargo features must be split correctly, add_counts guard must include encode_data. A stub/empty implementation would fail because tests verify stats code still exists (Part C of test_deadlock_pattern_removed checks .entry() still exists in cfg blocks).

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/next.js as a git repo, and the agent could read the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to see exactly what patterns are checked. The agent could also grep for 'TaskCacheStats::task_name' or 'FormatSizes' in the test file and reverse-engineer the expected patterns. No anti-cheating protections exist — solution/solve.sh is accessible if present in the container.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All four required changes from instruction.md are tested: deadlock fix (test_deadlock_pattern_removed, test_task_name_from_storage), feature split (test_cargo_feature_split), add_counts guard (test_add_counts_covers_data), and formatting struct (test_format_sizes_struct).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_deadlock_pattern_removed, test_task_name_from_storage, test_cargo_feature_split, etc.), grouped with clear section comments ([static] pass_to_pass, [pr_diff] fail_to_pass), and each has a detailed docstring explaining what it checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data output (JSON/CSV/API/DB).

no_typos.reason: File paths, feature names, and variable names in instruction.md match those used in tests and solution without typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md names the exact method 'persist_snapshot', the exact pattern '.entry(self.get_task_name(task_id, turbo_tasks))', the exact struct 'TaskCacheStats', and directs to specific cfg blocks and the 'process' closure. It also specifies exact replacement logic ('derived from the storage reference directly') — this goes well beyond identifying the buggy module.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The Cargo.toml diff is localized to the [features] section, adding one line and modifying one line. It doesn't contradict other files.
